rules.suggestion: Avoid long operations (network, database, file I/O) on the main thread.
rules.devSuggestion: Use AsyncTask, coroutines (Dispatchers.IO), or WorkManager for background work. Check for deadlock or lock contention in synchronized blocks. Use StrictMode to detect accidental main-thread I/O during development.

rules.suggestion: Check for null references before accessing objects.
rules.devSuggestion: Enable Kotlin null-safety (val?: operator). Add null checks or use Optional. Initialize fields in constructors or @PostConstruct. Audit Intent extras, Bundle values, and cursor results for null.

rules.suggestion: Reduce memory usage or image sizes.
rules.devSuggestion: Use BitmapFactory.Options.inSampleSize for image downsampling. Load large files in chunks via streams. Avoid holding references to Activities/Contexts in static fields. Profile with Android Studio Memory Profiler. Increase largeHeap if justified.

rules.suggestion: Check for infinite recursion in method calls.
rules.devSuggestion: Verify all recursive methods have a valid base case. Check mutual recursion (A calls B calls A). Use iterative alternatives or explicit stacks for deep traversals. Set a recursion depth limit if appropriate.

rules.suggestion: Check that all required permissions are granted.
rules.devSuggestion: Request runtime permissions before accessing protected APIs. Handle Permission Revocation gracefully (re-request or show rationale). Check for signature-level or system-level permission requirements. Verify AndroidManifest.xml declarations.
Use 'is' checks or safe casts (as?) in Kotlin. Avoid raw types and unchecked casts with generics. Verify serialization/deserialization type consistency. Check Intent/Bundle extras before casting.

rules.suggestion: Check component lifecycle state before performing operations.
rules.devSuggestion: Ensure Fragment/Activity is in correct lifecycle state (e.g., don't commit after onSaveInstanceState). Use viewLifecycleOwner for Fragment view operations. Add state guards before state-dependent API calls.

rules.suggestion: Move network calls to a background thread.
rules.devSuggestion: Use coroutines with Dispatchers.IO, Retrofit with enqueue(), or OkHttp's async API. Wrap network calls in withContext(Dispatchers.IO). StrictMode can catch this during development.

Verify table schema matches your queries (column names, types). Add incremental migrations in Room/onUpgrade. Handle database corruption with Room.databaseBuilder.fallbackToDestructiveMigration(). Use transactions for batch operations.

Check storage permissions (READ/WRITE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE, scoped storage). Handle FileNotFoundException and EOFException gracefully. Use try-with-resources (use{} in Kotlin) to close streams. Verify network availability before network I/O.
